description: UART characters arrive as garbage that worsens at higher rates because the two endpoints run at different effective baud rates.
body: Failure mode: a divisor rounding error or wrong clock source leaves one side at a slightly different baud, accumulating bit-sampling error across each frame. Detection signals: framing errors counting up, corruption that grows with frame length, and a fixed offset that disappears at lower baud. Do not raise the baud rate while framing errors persist. Safer fallback: recompute the divisor against the real peripheral clock, verify the error margin, and switch to a crystal source if the on-chip oscillator drifts.
